interface: Terraform Enterprise REST API v2 (JSON:API) — configuration versions and runs (stable)

request_headers.Content-Type: application/vnd.api+json on every /api/v2 request that carries a body

create_configuration_version.auto_queue_rule: auto-queue-runs defaults to true; this launcher always sends false so the only run created is the one it creates explicitly

upload_archive.url: the configuration version's upload-url attribute, verbatim — a temporary pre-signed archivist URL that may live on a different host than the API
upload_archive.auth_rule: the upload URL is pre-signed; the request MUST NOT carry an Authorization header, on any host

show_configuration_version.wait_rule: poll until status is uploaded (ready for a run) or errored (terminal failure); pending and fetching keep polling

actions.is-confirmable: boolean — whether the run is waiting for confirmation

run_states.confirmable: any status whose actions.is-confirmable is true (typically planned, cost_estimated, policy_checked)
run_states.policy_override_rule: policy_override means a hard-stopped sentinel check awaits a manual override; this launcher never overrides policy, so it is a policy failure
run_states.failure_stage_rule: stage is 'policy' for policy_soft_failed and policy_override; otherwise 'apply' if the launcher already confirmed the run, else 'plan'

confirm_run.idempotency_rule: the launcher confirms a given run at most once

errors.client_rule: every non-2xx /api/v2 response decodes into a structured API error carrying the HTTP status code and the parsed errors array
errors.not_found_masking: 404 Not Found is returned both for resources that do not exist and for resources the token is not authorized to see

pacing.rule: no sleeps; between successive polls the client calls its injected pace hook, and a pace error (e.g. context cancellation) aborts the wait with that error
